name: pg108_single_color
description: PG108 — firmware-fired single-color M3 mode spitting (heat + spit + wipe + park).
diff_explanation: Round 2 cleaned up PG108: the motion path (heat -> waiting -> spit -> wipe -> park) is preserved verbatim. The trace shows one residual difference at the fan-restore line: baseline reads the LIVE `printer['fan_generic cooling_fan'].speed` and re-sets it (effectively a no-op since the fan didn't change), while current reads the CAPTURED PG104 value `PRZ_RUNTIME_STATE.cooling_fan_speed` and restores to that. In production both produce the same value because PG104 captures the live fan speed at print start; in this test scenario PG104 hasn't run, so the captured default (254) appears instead of the test's seeded live value (0.5). This unifies PG108's fan-restore pattern with the rest of the codebase (_SPITTING_FRAME, ORCA_PURGE all use captured PRZ_RUNTIME_STATE).
